body.title: CC shim: ack receipt + configurable nag loop
body.result: ack note + nag loop with external state; 7 tests added; restart recovery via nag_state/ flat files
body.description:
**Problem:** CCWorkerListener currently receives dispatch envelopes but doesn't ack receipt, doesn't update ticket state with a receipt timestamp, and has no nag loop to remind CC about pending assignments.

**What to build:**
1. On envelope receipt: send ack back to Granny's mailbox immediately. Add a note to the ticket: 'CC.0 acked at <iso-timestamp>'. Ticket observably shows when the shim received the assignment.
2. Nag loop: after acking, start a per-ticket timer. Every CC_SHIM_NAG_INTERVAL seconds (default: 600, i.e. 10 min), if the ticket is still not in_progress, fire: `tmux send-keys -t <CC_SESSION> $'\r\r\rcheck messages when possible\n'`. Stop nagging when ticket moves to in_progress or is cancelled/closed.
3. Session targeting: use CC_TMUX_SESSION env var, else hostname-based detection (existing _detect_session_name() logic).

**Affected files:** devices/granny/cc_worker_listener.py, possibly devices/claude/constants.py (session name)
**Design rules:** External state — timer/nag state must not be in-memory-only; if listener restarts, it re-checks ticket state and resumes nagging if still needed
**Scope boundary:** Ack + nag only. Priority/HALT path is T-cc-shim-priority-halt.
**Completion criteria:** Test shows: dispatch envelope arrives → ack sent → ticket note added with timestamp → nag fires at interval → nag stops when ticket goes in_progress.
